text: \( P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cap B) \). If \( P(A)=0.3, P(B)=0.4, P(A \cap B)=0.1 \), then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
name: Addition Theorem
subject: maths

text: The cross product \( \vec{a} \times \vec{b} \) gives a vector:
explanation: \( \vec{a}\times\vec{b} \) is perpendicular to both \(\vec{a}\) and \(\vec{b}\) (normal to their plane).
name: Cross Product Direction
subject: maths

text: In the complex [Fe(CN)₆]⁴⁻, the oxidation state of Fe is:
explanation: CN⁻ each has charge -1. Total charge on complex = -4. Let Fe = x: x + 6(-1) = -4 → x = +2. Fe(II) complex.
name: Oxidation State in Complex
subject: che

text: If two roots of \( x^3 - px^2 + qx - r = 0 \) are equal, say \( \alpha, \alpha, \beta \), then which condition must hold?
explanation: From Vieta's: \( 2\alpha+\beta=p \), \( \alpha^2+2\alpha\beta=q \), \( \alpha^2\beta=r \). When all three equal: \( p^3=27r \). All conditions can hold simultaneously.
name: Equal Roots in Cubic
subject: maths

text: A sigma (σ) bond is formed by:
options: Overlap of d and p orbitals only
explanation: σ bond: end-to-end (head-on) overlap along internuclear axis. π bond: lateral (sideways) overlap.
name: Sigma Bond Formation
subject: che

text: The apparent weight of a person in a lift accelerating upward is:
explanation: Apparent weight: \( W' = m(g+a) \) when accelerating up (heavier feeling). \( W' = m(g-a) \) when accelerating down.
name: Apparent Weight in Lift
subject: phy

text: The tangent to parabola \( y^2=4ax \) at point \((at^2, 2at)\) is:
explanation: Tangent at parametric point \((at^2,2at)\) on \(y^2=4ax\): \( ty = x + at^2 \)
name: Parametric Tangent to Parabola
subject: maths

text: Nuclear fusion requires:
options: Very high temperature (\( \sim 10^7\) K) to overcome Coulomb barrier
explanation: Fusion: two light nuclei (e.g., H isotopes) fuse to form heavier nucleus with large energy release. Requires ~10⁷ K for thermonuclear fusion. Powers the Sun.
name: Nuclear Fusion Condition
subject: phy

text: The shape of \( \text{PCl}_5 \) is:
explanation: \(\text{PCl}_5\): 5 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs, \(sp^3d\) hybridisation → trigonal bipyramidal.
name: Shape of PCl₅
subject: che

text: The energy stored in a capacitor is:
explanation: Energy in capacitor: \( U = \frac{1}{2}CV^2 = \frac{Q^2}{2C} = \frac{QV}{2} \). Stored as electric field energy.
name: Energy in Capacitor
subject: phy

text: The kinetic energy of rolling without slipping is:
explanation: Rolling KE = translational + rotational: \( KE = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 + \frac{1}{2}I\omega^2 \). For solid sphere: \( KE = \frac{7}{10}mv^2 \).
name: Rolling Kinetic Energy
subject: phy

text: The distance from point \( (x_1,y_1,z_1) \) to plane \( ax+by+cz+d=0 \) is:
explanation: Distance from point to plane: \( d = \frac{|ax_1+by_1+cz_1+d|}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2+c^2}} \)
name: Point to Plane Distance
subject: maths

text: If \( f(x) \) is an odd function, then \( \int_{-a}^{a} f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: Odd function property: \( \int_{-a}^{a}f(x)dx = 0 \) (symmetry about origin cancels)
name: Odd Function Integral
subject: maths

text: The general solution of \( \sin\theta = \sin\alpha \) is:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = \sin\alpha \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi + (-1)^n\alpha,\ n \in \mathbb{Z} \)
name: General Solution sinθ=sinα
subject: maths

text: The intensity of an electromagnetic wave is related to field amplitudes by:
explanation: EM wave intensity: \( I = \frac{1}{2}c\epsilon_0 E_0^2 = \frac{c B_0^2}{2\mu_0} \). Also \( E_0/B_0 = c \).
name: EM Wave Intensity
subject: phy

text: In the reaction \( 2\text{H}_2 + \text{O}_2 \to 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \), 4 g of \( \text{H}_2 \) reacts with how many grams of \( \text{O}_2 \)?
explanation: 4 g \(\text{H}_2\) = 2 mol. From stoichiometry, 2 mol \(\text{H}_2\) needs 1 mol \(\text{O}_2\) = 32 g.
name: Stoichiometry Calculation
subject: che

text: The inradius \(r\) of a triangle is:
explanation: Inradius \( r = \frac{\Delta}{s} \) where \( s = \frac{a+b+c}{2} \) is the semi-perimeter.
name: Inradius Formula
subject: maths

text: A p-n junction diode conducts in:
options: Forward bias (forward current flows when V > knee voltage ~0.7V for Si)
explanation: Forward bias: p-side to + terminal. Reduces barrier → significant current flows. Reverse bias: increases barrier → only tiny leakage current.
name: Diode Conduction
subject: phy

text: The Joule-Thomson effect describes:
options: Cooling of gas on adiabatic expansion through a porous plug
Expansion of gas at constant temperature
explanation: Joule-Thomson effect: real gas cools on adiabatic expansion through a porous plug (below inversion temperature).
name: Joule-Thomson Effect
subject: che

text: If \( |A| = 5 \) and \( A \) is \( 3 \times 3 \), then \( |\text{adj}(A)| = \)
name: Adjoint Determinant
subject: maths

text: Bleaching powder contains:
explanation: Bleaching powder: Ca(OCl)Cl (mixed salt of Ca²⁺ with OCl⁻ and Cl⁻). Formula: CaOCl₂ or Ca(OCl)Cl. Made by Cl₂ + Ca(OH)₂.
name: Bleaching Powder
subject: che

text: \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x = \)
explanation: Definition of \(e\): \( \lim_{x\to\infty}\left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x = e \approx 2.718 \)
name: Definition of e
subject: maths

text: Acid rain is caused primarily by:
options: SO₂ and NOₓ reacting with water to form H₂SO₄ and HNO₃
explanation: Acid rain: SO₂ + H₂O + ½O₂ → H₂SO₄; NOₓ + H₂O → HNO₃. pH < 5.6. Damages buildings (marble), kills fish, harms forests.
name: Acid Rain Causes
subject: che

text: For a projectile, the horizontal range is maximum when the angle of projection is:
explanation: Range \( R = \frac{u^2\sin 2\theta}{g} \). Maximum when \( \sin 2\theta = 1 \Rightarrow 2\theta = 90° \Rightarrow \theta = 45° \).
name: Maximum Range of Projectile
subject: phy

text: The standard equation of an ellipse with semi-major axis \(a\) along x-axis is:
explanation: Ellipse: \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) with \(a>b>0\). Foci at \( (\pm c,0) \) where \( c^2=a^2-b^2 \).
name: Ellipse Standard Form
subject: maths

text: The molar conductivity \( \Lambda_m \) is related to conductivity \( \kappa \) and concentration \(C\) by:
explanation: \( \Lambda_m = \frac{\kappa \times 1000}{C} \) (when \(C\) in mol/L and \(\kappa\) in S/cm). Units: S·cm²/mol.
name: Molar Conductivity Formula
subject: che

text: The Mean Value Theorem guarantees \( \exists\, c \in (a,b) \) such that:
explanation: Lagrange's MVT: \( f'(c) = \frac{f(b)-f(a)}{b-a} \) for some \(c \in (a,b)\).
name: Mean Value Theorem
subject: maths

text: If the sum of two roots of \( x^3 + px^2 + qx + r = 0 \) is zero, then:
explanation: Let roots be \( \alpha, -\alpha, \beta \). Then \( \alpha+(-\alpha)+\beta = -p \Rightarrow \beta=-p \). Since \(\beta\) is root: use Vieta's relations to get \(pq = r\).
name: Condition on Roots Sum
subject: maths

text: The order of the DE representing family of circles \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \) (r arbitrary) is:
explanation: One arbitrary constant \(r\) → eliminate by differentiating once → order 1.
name: Order from Family of Curves
subject: maths

text: The general solution of \( \sin\theta = 0 \) is:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = 0 \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi \), where \( n \in \mathbb{Z} \).
name: General Solution of sinθ=0
subject: maths

text: Beats are produced when two sound waves of frequencies \(f_1\) and \(f_2\) are superposed. Beat frequency equals:
explanation: Beat frequency \( f_{beat} = |f_1 - f_2| \). Perceptible as beats when difference is small (< ~7 Hz for human ear).
name: Beat Frequency
subject: phy

text: The process of superimposing a low-frequency signal on a high-frequency carrier wave is called:
explanation: Modulation: signal wave is combined with carrier wave. Necessary because low-frequency signals cannot be transmitted as EM waves efficiently over long distances.
name: Modulation Definition
subject: phy

text: Coherent sources must have:
options: Same phase difference (constant phase relationship) and same frequency
explanation: Coherent sources: same frequency and a constant (zero or fixed) phase difference. Required for sustained interference pattern.
name: Coherent Sources
subject: phy

text: The value of \( \cosh 0 = \)
name: cosh(0)
subject: maths

text: The symmetric form of a line through \((x_1,y_1,z_1)\) with direction ratios \((a,b,c)\) is:
explanation: Symmetric form: \( \frac{x-x_1}{a}=\frac{y-y_1}{b}=\frac{z-z_1}{c} = \lambda \)
name: Symmetric Form of Line
subject: maths

explanation: Standard formula: \( \int \frac{1}{a^2+x^2}dx = \frac{1}{a}\tan^{-1}\frac{x}{a}+C \)
name: Standard Form Integral
subject: maths

text: Weightlessness in a satellite is because:
options: Both satellite and person are in free fall (same centripetal acceleration)
explanation: In orbit: both person and satellite have same centripetal acceleration. Person is in continuous free fall → apparent weightlessness.
name: Weightlessness in Orbit
subject: phy

text: The sum of slopes of lines \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) is:
explanation: If \(m_1,m_2\) are slopes: \( m_1+m_2 = -\frac{2h}{b} \) and \( m_1m_2 = \frac{a}{b} \) (Vieta's).
name: Sum of Slopes
subject: maths

text: The directrix of parabola \( y^2=4ax \) is:
explanation: For \( y^2=4ax \): directrix is the vertical line \( x = -a \).
name: Directrix of Parabola
subject: maths

text: For a second-order reaction \( r = k[A]^2 \), the integrated rate law is:
explanation: Second-order integrated law: \( \frac{1}{[A]} = \frac{1}{[A]_0} + kt \). Plot of \(1/[A]\) vs \(t\) is linear.
name: Second Order Rate Law
subject: che

text: At absolute zero (0 K), the kinetic energy of ideal gas molecules is:
explanation: At \( T = 0\,\text{K} \): \( KE = \frac{3}{2}kT = 0 \). All molecular motion ceases. However, this is unattainable (Third Law of Thermodynamics).
name: KE at Absolute Zero
subject: phy

text: In the expansion of \( (a+b)^n \), if \( n = 9 \), then how many middle terms are there?
explanation: When \(n\) is odd, there are 2 middle terms: \( T_{\frac{n+1}{2}} \) and \( T_{\frac{n+3}{2}} \), i.e., \(T_5\) and \(T_6\).
name: Middle Terms for Odd n
subject: maths

text: Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky (HVZ) reaction introduces halogen at _____ position of carboxylic acids:
explanation: HVZ reaction: RCOOH + X₂ (PCl₃/PBr₃ catalyst) → α-halocarboxylic acid. X introduced at α-carbon of the acid.
name: HVZ Reaction
subject: che

text: The entropy change \(\Delta S\) for a reversible process at temperature \(T\) is:
explanation: Entropy change: \( dS = dQ_{rev}/T \), or \( \Delta S = Q/T \) for isothermal reversible process. For reversible adiabatic: \( \Delta S = 0 \).
name: Entropy Change
subject: phy

text: The property \( \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = \int_a^b f(a+b-x)\,dx \) is used to evaluate:
options: Integrals that are difficult to solve directly
explanation: This \(x \to a+b-x\) substitution simplifies integrals like \( \int_0^{\pi} \frac{x\sin x}{1+\cos^2 x}dx \).
name: King's Property
subject: maths

text: A catalyst in a chemical equilibrium:
options: Increases rate of both forward and reverse reactions equally
Only increases rate of forward reaction
explanation: A catalyst increases rates of both forward and reverse reactions equally. It does not change K or shift equilibrium position.
name: Catalyst and Equilibrium
subject: che

text: The angle bisectors of \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) are given by:
explanation: Equation of angle bisectors: \( \frac{x^2-y^2}{a-b} = \frac{xy}{h} \)
name: Angle Bisectors of Pair
subject: maths

text: An ideal solution obeys Raoult's law over the entire concentration range and has:
explanation: Ideal solution: \( \Delta H_{mix} = 0 \), \( \Delta V_{mix} = 0 \). Example: benzene + toluene.
name: Ideal Solution Properties
subject: che

text: \( 2^n \geq n^2 \) holds for all \( n \geq \)
explanation: Check: \(n=3\): \(8 < 9\) (fails), \(n=4\): \(16 = 16\) (holds), \(n \geq 4\): true by induction.
name: Exponential vs Polynomial
subject: maths

text: The slope of line \( 3x - 4y + 7 = 0 \) is:
explanation: Rewrite: \( y = \frac{3}{4}x + \frac{7}{4} \). Slope \( m = \frac{3}{4} \).
name: Slope from General Equation
subject: maths

text: For parametric equations \( x=f(t), y=g(t) \), \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \)
explanation: Parametric differentiation: \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{dy/dt}{dx/dt} = \frac{g'(t)}{f'(t)} \)
name: Parametric Differentiation
subject: maths

text: Lenz's law states that the induced current always:
options: Flows in the same direction as the change
Opposes the cause that produces it (by Faraday's law)
Flows from low to high potential
explanation: Lenz's law: induced current direction is such that its magnetic effect opposes the change in flux causing it. Conservation of energy.
name: Lenz's Law
subject: phy

text: The modulus-amplitude form of \( z = -\sqrt{3} + i \) is:
explanation: \( r = \sqrt{3+1} = 2 \). Angle in 2nd quadrant: \( \theta = \pi - \frac{\pi}{6} = \frac{5\pi}{6} \)
name: Modulus-Amplitude Form
subject: maths

text: Which of the following is NOT a colligative property?
explanation: Optical rotation depends on the nature of solute (chiral molecules), not on the number of particles.
name: Non-Colligative Property
subject: che

text: The second law of thermodynamics implies:
options: Heat cannot spontaneously flow from cold to hot body (Clausius statement)
explanation: 2nd Law (Clausius): heat cannot spontaneously flow from cold to hot. (Kelvin-Planck): impossible to convert all heat to work.
name: Second Law of Thermodynamics
subject: phy

text: The modern periodic law states that properties of elements are periodic functions of their:
explanation: Moseley's Modern Periodic Law: Properties are periodic functions of atomic number (not mass as Mendeleev stated).
name: Modern Periodic Law
subject: che

text: The product formula \( 2\sin A \cos B = \)
explanation: Product-to-Sum: \( 2\sin A\cos B = \sin(A+B) + \sin(A-B) \)
name: Product-to-Sum
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{1}{x(x+1)} = \frac{A}{x} + \frac{B}{x+1} \). What are \(A\) and \(B\)?
explanation: At \(x=0\): \(1 = A(1) \Rightarrow A=1\). At \(x=-1\): \(1 = B(-1) \Rightarrow B=-1\).
name: Basic Partial Fraction
subject: maths

text: Which of the following is the correct order of acidic strength of hydrocarbons?
explanation: Acidic strength: alkyne (sp C, more electronegative) > alkene (sp²) > alkane (sp³). pKa: alkyne ≈ 25, alkene ≈ 44, alkane ≈ 50.
name: Acidity of Hydrocarbons
subject: che

text: The height \(h\) of liquid rise in a capillary tube of radius \(r\) is:
explanation: Capillary rise: \( h = \frac{2T\cos\theta}{\rho g r} \). Water (\(\theta<90°\)) rises; mercury (\(\theta>90°\)) depresses.
name: Capillary Rise Formula
subject: phy

text: Ferromagnetism disappears above the Curie temperature \(T_C\) because:
options: Thermal energy disrupts domain alignment → paramagnetic behaviour
explanation: Above Curie temperature: thermal energy > domain exchange energy → magnetic domains randomise → ferromagnetism disappears, material becomes paramagnetic.
name: Curie Temperature
subject: phy

text: \( \sin C + \sin D = \)
explanation: Sum-to-Product: \( \sin C + \sin D = 2\sin\frac{C+D}{2}\cos\frac{C-D}{2} \)
name: Sum-to-Product Formula
subject: maths

text: Fehling's solution is reduced by:
explanation: Fehling's test: gives brick-red Cu₂O precipitate with aliphatic aldehydes. Aromatic aldehydes (benzaldehyde) do NOT reduce Fehling's. Ketones: negative.
name: Fehling's Test
subject: che

text: The maximum value of \( f(x) = \sin x + \cos x \) is:
explanation: \( f(x) = \sqrt{2}\sin(x+\frac{\pi}{4}) \). Maximum \( = \sqrt{2} \) when \( x = \frac{\pi}{4} \).
name: Maximum of sin+cos
subject: maths

text: The magnetic field at the centre of a circular loop of radius \(R\) carrying current \(I\) is:
explanation: Centre of circular loop: \( B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2R} \). For N turns: \( B = \frac{\mu_0 NI}{2R} \).
name: Field at Centre of Circular Loop
subject: phy

text: The value of \( \binom{2n}{n} \) grows approximately as:
explanation: By Stirling's approximation: \( \binom{2n}{n} \sim \frac{4^n}{\sqrt{\pi n}} \) for large \(n\).
name: Central Binomial Coefficient
subject: maths

text: The coefficient of restitution \(e\) for a perfectly elastic collision is:
explanation: \( e = \frac{\text{relative speed of separation}}{\text{relative speed of approach}} \). Perfectly elastic: \(e=1\), Perfectly inelastic: \(e=0\).
name: Coefficient of Restitution
subject: phy

text: The Swartz reaction converts alkyl chlorides/bromides to:
options: Alkyl fluorides using CoF₂ or SbF₃
explanation: Swartz reaction: R–Cl + AgF → R–F + AgCl. Or with CoF₂, SbF₃. Used to make alkyl fluorides (direct fluorination is too vigorous).
name: Swartz Reaction
subject: che

text: If \( A = \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{pmatrix} \), then \( |A| = \)
name: 2x2 Determinant
subject: maths

text: Vitamins that are fat-soluble are:
explanation: Fat-soluble: A, D, E, K (stored in body fat and liver). Water-soluble: B-complex and C (must be taken regularly). Excess fat-soluble → toxicity possible.
name: Fat-Soluble Vitamins
subject: che

text: The mobility \(\mu\) of charge carriers is:
options: \( \mu = v_d / E \) (drift velocity per unit electric field)
explanation: Mobility: \( \mu = v_d/E = e\tau/m \). Units: m²/(V·s). Conductivity \( \sigma = ne\mu \).
name: Carrier Mobility
subject: phy

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}\int_a^{g(x)}f(t)\,dt = \)
explanation: Leibniz rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}\int_a^{g(x)}f(t)dt = f(g(x))\cdot g'(x) \) (chain rule applied).
name: Leibniz Rule
subject: maths

text: \( \tan^{-1}x + \tan^{-1}y = \tan^{-1}\frac{x+y}{1-xy} \) when:
explanation: This formula holds when \( xy < 1 \). When \(xy > 1\), add or subtract \(\pi\) depending on sign of \(x\).
name: Addition Formula Condition
subject: maths

text: The entropy change for isothermal reversible expansion of ideal gas is:
explanation: \( \Delta S = nR\ln\frac{V_2}{V_1} = -nR\ln\frac{P_2}{P_1} \) for isothermal ideal gas expansion.
name: Entropy of Isothermal Expansion
subject: che

text: In triangle \(ABC\), \( \tan\frac{A}{2} = \)
explanation: \( \tan\frac{A}{2} = \frac{r}{s-a} = \sqrt{\frac{(s-b)(s-c)}{s(s-a)}} \). Both forms are equivalent.
name: Half-angle Tangent
subject: maths

text: The trend in ionic radius for isoelectronic species (same electron configuration) with increasing nuclear charge:
explanation: For isoelectronic species (e.g., N³⁻, O²⁻, F⁻, Na⁺, Mg²⁺, Al³⁺): higher nuclear charge → smaller ionic radius.
name: Isoelectronic Ionic Radius
subject: che

text: The half-life of a first-order reaction is:
explanation: First-order half-life: \( t_{1/2} = \frac{\ln 2}{k} = \frac{0.693}{k} \). Independent of initial concentration.
name: First Order Half-life
subject: che

text: The acceleration due to gravity \(g\) varies with height \(h\) above Earth's surface as:
explanation: \( g' = \frac{g}{(1+h/R)^2} \approx g\left(1 - \frac{2h}{R}\right) \) for \( h \ll R \). Decreases with height.
name: g at Height h
subject: phy

text: For two circles with centers \( d \) apart and radii \( r_1, r_2 \), they are externally tangent when:
explanation: External tangency: \( d = r_1 + r_2 \). Internal tangency: \( d = |r_1 - r_2| \).
name: External Tangency
subject: maths

text: The general solution of \( \cos 2\theta = 0 \) is:
explanation: \( \cos 2\theta = 0 \Rightarrow 2\theta = (2n+1)\frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow \theta = \frac{(2n+1)\pi}{4} \)
name: cos 2θ = 0
subject: maths

text: The cofactor \( C_{11} \) of matrix \( \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 0 & 4 & 5 \\ 1 & 0 & 6 \end{pmatrix} \) is:
name: Cofactor Calculation
subject: maths

text: Two circles are orthogonal if:
explanation: Orthogonality condition: \( 2g_1g_2+2f_1f_2 = c_1+c_2 \)
name: Orthogonal Circles
subject: maths

text: \( \int e^x\,dx = \)
explanation: \( \int e^x\,dx = e^x+C \)
name: Integral of eˣ
subject: maths

text: Both roots of \( x^2 - 6x + k = 0 \) are positive if:
explanation: Need \( D \geq 0 \Rightarrow k \leq 9 \), sum \(=6>0\), product \(=k>0\). So \( 0 < k \leq 9 \). Strictly positive roots: \( 0 < k < 9 \).
name: Both Roots Positive
subject: maths

text: \( \hat{i}\times\hat{j} = \)
explanation: \( \hat{i}\times\hat{j}=\hat{k},\ \hat{j}\times\hat{k}=\hat{i},\ \hat{k}\times\hat{i}=\hat{j} \). Cyclic order.
name: Unit Vector Cross Products
subject: maths

text: The coefficient of \( x^5 \) in \( (1+x)^{10} \) is:
name: Coefficient of x^5
subject: maths

text: The conversion of phenol to cyclohexanol requires:
explanation: Phenol → cyclohexanol by catalytic hydrogenation: PhOH + 3H₂ (Pt/Pd, high pressure) → cyclohexanol. The aromatic ring is reduced.
name: Phenol to Cyclohexanol
subject: che

text: Plaster of Paris is:
explanation: Plaster of Paris: \( \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} \) (hemi-hydrate). Sets by absorbing water: \( \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} + \frac{3}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} \to \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \).
name: Plaster of Paris
subject: che

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\ln x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\ln x) = \frac{1}{x} \) for \( x > 0 \).
name: Derivative of ln x
subject: maths

text: Faraday's first law of electrolysis states:
options: Mass deposited is independent of current
Mass deposited ∝ charge passed \( (m = ZQ = ZIt) \)
explanation: Faraday's First Law: \( m = Z \cdot Q = Z \cdot I \cdot t \), where \(Z\) = electrochemical equivalent.
name: Faraday's First Law
subject: che

text: The electric flux through a surface is maximum when the surface is:
explanation: \( \Phi = EA\cos\theta \). Maximum when \( \theta = 0 \) (field perpendicular to surface, i.e., area vector parallel to \(\vec{E}\)).
name: Maximum Electric Flux
subject: phy

text: For ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \), eccentricity \( e = \)
explanation: Eccentricity of ellipse: \( e = \frac{c}{a} \) where \( c = \sqrt{a^2-b^2} \). Always \( 0 < e < 1 \).
name: Eccentricity of Ellipse
subject: maths

text: PMMA (Plexiglas, Perspex) is a polymer of:
explanation: PMMA: addition polymerisation of methyl methacrylate. Transparent, used as glass substitute (Plexiglas). Also called acrylic glass.
name: PMMA Monomer
subject: che

text: The maximum number of electrons in a shell with principal quantum number \(n\) is:
explanation: Maximum electrons in nth shell \( = 2n^2 \). Shell 1: 2, Shell 2: 8, Shell 3: 18.
name: Electrons per Shell
subject: che

text: In the measurement \( 2.35 \pm 0.01 \) cm, the relative error is:
explanation: Relative (fractional) error \( = \frac{\Delta a}{a} = \frac{0.01}{2.35} \approx 0.0043 = 0.43\% \).
name: Relative Error
subject: phy

text: The basicity order of amines in aqueous solution is:
explanation: In aqueous solution (alkyl amines): 2° > 1° > 3° > NH₃. 3° has steric hindrance reducing solvation of the conjugate acid. 2° has best balance.
name: Basicity Order of Amines
subject: che

text: Standard deviation is the _____ of variance:
explanation: \( \sigma = \sqrt{\text{Variance}} \). SD is positive square root of variance.
name: SD and Variance Relation
subject: maths

text: The de Broglie wavelength of a particle of momentum \(p\) is:
explanation: de Broglie hypothesis: \( \lambda = h/p = h/mv \). All matter has wave nature. Davisson-Germer experiment confirmed this for electrons.
name: de Broglie Wavelength
subject: phy

text: The projection of \( \vec{a} \) on \( \vec{b} \) is:
explanation: Projection of \(\vec{a}\) on \(\vec{b}\): \( \frac{\vec{a}\cdot\vec{b}}{|\vec{b}|} = \vec{a}\cdot\hat{b} \). Both forms are equivalent.
name: Projection of Vector
subject: maths

text: The partial fraction form of \( \frac{f(x)}{(x-a)^2(x-b)} \) is:
explanation: For repeated linear factor \((x-a)^2\), decompose as \( \frac{A}{x-a} + \frac{B}{(x-a)^2} \), plus \( \frac{C}{x-b} \).
name: Repeated Linear Factors
subject: maths

text: The mirror formula is:
explanation: Mirror formula: \( \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{u} = \frac{1}{f} = \frac{2}{R} \). Sign convention: distances measured from pole; positive in direction of incident light.
name: Mirror Formula
subject: phy

text: The number of handshakes when 10 people each shake hands with every other is:
explanation: Each handshake involves 2 people: \( ^{10}C_2 = 45 \)
name: Handshakes Problem
subject: maths

text: The eddy currents in a conductor placed in a changing magnetic field:
options: Produce heat (energy dissipation) and oppose the change (useful in braking)
explanation: Eddy currents: closed loop currents in bulk conductor in changing \(B\). Cause: heating (loss in motors/transformers). Uses: induction furnace, magnetic braking, speedometers.
name: Eddy Currents
subject: phy

text: A function is said to have a removable discontinuity at \(x=a\) if:
options: \( f(a) \) is undefined but limit exists
explanation: Removable discontinuity: \( \lim_{x\to a}f(x) \) exists but either \(f(a)\) is undefined or \( f(a)\neq\lim f(x) \).
name: Removable Discontinuity
subject: maths

text: Pauli's exclusion principle states that no two electrons in an atom can have:
explanation: Pauli's Exclusion Principle: No two electrons in an atom can have the same set of all four quantum numbers.
name: Pauli Exclusion Principle
subject: che

text: If one root of \( x^2 - 5x + k = 0 \) is 2, then \( k = \)
explanation: Substituting \( x=2 \): \( 4 - 10 + k = 0 \Rightarrow k = 6 \)
name: Finding k from Root
subject: maths

text: The industrial preparation of hydrogen by steam reforming of methane gives:
explanation: Steam reforming: \( \text{CH}_4 + \text{H}_2\text{O} \xrightarrow{\text{Ni, 1270K}} \text{CO} + 3\text{H}_2 \) (synthesis gas/water gas). Most H₂ is produced this way.
name: Steam Reforming
subject: che

text: The number of arbitrary constants in a general solution equals:
explanation: General solution of an nth-order DE contains exactly \(n\) arbitrary constants.
name: Constants in General Solution
subject: maths

text: The radius of gyration \(k\) is defined by:
explanation: Radius of gyration: \( k = \sqrt{I/M} \), so \( I = Mk^2 \). It represents the effective distance of the mass from the axis.
name: Radius of Gyration
subject: phy

text: The argument of \( z = -1 - i \) is:
explanation: \( z = -1-i \) lies in 3rd quadrant. \( \arg(z) = -(\pi - \frac{\pi}{4}) = -\frac{3\pi}{4} \)
name: Argument of Complex Number
subject: maths

text: The second derivative of \( f(x) = x^3 \) is:
name: Second Derivative
subject: maths

text: The hybridisation of carbon in benzene is:
explanation: Benzene: each C is sp² hybridised, forming 3 σ bonds. Unhybridised p orbitals overlap to form delocalised π system.
name: Hybridisation in Benzene
subject: che

text: In \( (2x - 3y)^5 \), the sum of coefficients (setting \(x=y=1\)) is:
name: Sum of Coefficients
subject: maths

text: The speed of a transverse wave on a string with tension \(T\) and linear mass density \(\mu\) is:
explanation: Wave speed on string: \( v = \sqrt{T/\mu} \). Higher tension or lower mass density → faster wave.
name: Wave Speed on String
subject: phy

text: The coordination number in a BCC structure is:
explanation: BCC: each atom touches 8 nearest neighbours (body centre touches all 8 corners). CN = 8.
name: BCC Coordination Number
subject: che

text: The equation of tangent to \( y=f(x) \) at \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
name: Equation of Tangent
subject: maths

text: If \( f(x) \) is an even function, then \( \int_{-a}^{a} f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: Even function property: \( \int_{-a}^{a}f(x)dx = 2\int_0^a f(x)dx \)
name: Even Function Integral
subject: maths

text: The incenter of triangle with vertices \( A, B, C \) and opposite sides \( a, b, c \) is:
explanation: Incenter \( = \left(\frac{ax_A+bx_B+cx_C}{a+b+c}, \frac{ay_A+by_B+cy_C}{a+b+c}\right) \) (weighted by opposite side lengths).
name: Incenter Formula
subject: maths

text: The meter bridge works on the principle of:
explanation: Metre bridge: balanced Wheatstone bridge using a uniform resistance wire of 100 cm. \( R/X = l/(100-l) \) gives unknown resistance X.
name: Metre Bridge
subject: phy

text: In \( \frac{1}{x^2-1} = \frac{A}{x-1} + \frac{B}{x+1} \), then \( A + B = \)
name: Sum of Constants
subject: maths

text: Transition elements are defined as elements that have:
options: Partially filled d orbitals in ground state or common oxidation state
explanation: Transition elements: partially filled d orbitals in ground state OR any common oxidation state. Zn, Cd, Hg are border cases.
name: Definition of Transition Elements
subject: che

text: The maximum value of \( -x^2 + 4x - 3 \) is:
explanation: Vertex at \( x = -\frac{b}{2a} = 2 \). Max value \( = -(4) + 8 - 3 = 1 \)
name: Maximum of Quadratic
subject: maths

text: The electron in hydrogen at n=2 has energy:
name: Energy at n=2
subject: phy

text: Young's modulus is defined as:
explanation: Young's modulus: \( Y = \frac{F/A}{\Delta L/L} = \frac{FL}{A\Delta L} \). Units: Pa = N/m². Measure of stiffness in tension.
name: Young's Modulus
subject: phy

text: Gold number is a measure of:
explanation: Gold number: minimum mass (in mg) of protective colloid to prevent coagulation of 10 mL gold sol by 1 mL 10% NaCl. Lower gold number = better protection.
name: Gold Number
subject: che

text: Equipotential surfaces are always:
explanation: Equipotential surfaces: \( V = \text{const} \). Always perpendicular to \(\vec{E}\). For point charge: concentric spheres. For uniform field: parallel planes.
name: Equipotential Surfaces
subject: phy

text: If \( y = x^x \), then \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \)
explanation: Take \(\ln\): \( \ln y = x\ln x \). Differentiate: \( \frac{y'}{y} = \ln x+1 \Rightarrow y' = x^x(1+\ln x) \).
name: Logarithmic Differentiation
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( 2x^2 - 5x + 3 = 0 \), then \( \alpha + \beta = \)
explanation: Sum of roots \( = -\frac{b}{a} = -\frac{-5}{2} = \frac{5}{2} \)
name: Sum of Roots
subject: maths

text: The value of \( \cos^{-1}(0) = \)
name: cos⁻¹(0)
subject: maths

text: The value of \( \sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = \)
explanation: Pythagorean identity: \( \sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = 1 \) for all \(\theta\).
name: Pythagorean Identity
subject: maths

text: The IUPAC name of [Co(NH₃)₆]Cl₃ is:
explanation: IUPAC: ligands named first (hexaammine), then metal with oxidation state (cobalt(III)), then outer anion (chloride). = Hexaamminecobalt(III) chloride.
name: IUPAC Nomenclature
subject: che

text: The Hall effect is used to determine:
options: Sign of charge carriers and carrier density: \( R_H = 1/(ne) \)
explanation: Hall effect: when current-carrying conductor is in \(\vec{B}\), transverse voltage (Hall voltage) develops. Determines sign of carriers and \( n = 1/(eR_H) \).
name: Hall Effect
subject: phy

text: The magnifying power of a simple microscope at near point (D=25 cm) is:
explanation: Simple microscope: \( M = 1 + D/f \) (image at near point). \( M = D/f \) (image at infinity). Near point \(D = 25\) cm.
name: Magnifying Power of Simple Microscope
subject: phy

text: The current gain \(\alpha\) in common base configuration is:
explanation: CB configuration: \( \alpha = I_C/I_E < 1 \). Relation: \( \beta = \alpha/(1-\alpha) \) or \( \alpha = \beta/(1+\beta) \). \( I_E = I_C + I_B \).
name: Common Base Current Gain
subject: phy

text: The angle between lines with slopes \( m_1 \) and \( m_2 \) is \( \tan\theta = \)
explanation: \( \tan\theta = \left|\frac{m_1-m_2}{1+m_1m_2}\right| \)
name: Angle Between Lines
subject: maths

explanation: Sum of first \(n\) odd numbers \( = n^2 \). Verify by induction.
name: Sum of Odd Numbers
subject: maths

text: In \( \text{SF}_6 \), the hybridisation of S is:
explanation: \(\text{SF}_6\): 6 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs → \(sp^3d^2\) hybridisation → octahedral shape.
name: Hybridisation in SF₆
subject: che

text: The reaction of HCHO (formaldehyde) with excess NaOH is:
explanation: HCHO has no α-H → Cannizzaro: 2HCHO + NaOH → CH₃OH (methanol) + HCOONa (sodium formate). Cross-Cannizzaro also possible.
name: HCHO Cannizzaro
subject: che

text: Number of ways to arrange 5 books on a shelf is:
explanation: Arrangements of 5 distinct items \( = 5! = 120 \)
name: Arrangements
subject: maths

text: The condition for constructive interference is path difference:
explanation: Constructive interference: \( \Delta = n\lambda \) (n = 0,±1,±2,...). Destructive: \( \Delta = (2n+1)\lambda/2 \).
name: Constructive Interference YDSE
subject: phy

text: Two coins are tossed. \( P(\text{exactly one head}) = \)
explanation: Sample space: \{HH, HT, TH, TT\}. Exactly one head: \{HT, TH\}. \( P = \frac{2}{4} = \frac{1}{2} \)
name: Coin Toss Probability
subject: maths

text: The coefficient of variation (CV) is calculated as:
explanation: \( CV = \frac{\sigma}{\bar{x}} \times 100 \). Used to compare relative variability.
name: Coefficient of Variation
subject: maths

text: In SHM, the total mechanical energy is:
explanation: Total energy in SHM: \( E = \frac{1}{2}kA^2 = \frac{1}{2}m\omega^2A^2 \). Constant (independent of position). \( E \propto A^2 \).
name: Total Energy in SHM
subject: phy

text: The area of triangle with \( a=5, b=6, c=7 \) using Heron's formula (\(s=9\)) is:
explanation: \( s=9 \). \( \Delta = \sqrt{9 \cdot 4 \cdot 3 \cdot 2} = \sqrt{216} = 6\sqrt{6} \). Both A and B are correct.
name: Heron's Calculation
subject: maths

text: If \( \omega \) is a complex cube root of unity, then \( \omega^{100} + \omega^{200} + \omega^{300} = \)
explanation: \( 100 = 3\times33+1,\ 200=3\times66+2,\ 300=3\times100 \). So \( \omega^1 + \omega^2 + 1 = 0+1 \)... Correction: \( \omega^{300} = (\omega^3)^{100} = 1 \), \( \omega^{100}=\omega \), \( \omega^{200}=\omega^2 \). Sum \( = 1+\omega+\omega^2+1-1 = 3\cdot1 \) for this pattern gives 3.
name: Omega Powers Sum
subject: maths

text: In which case is work done against gravity zero?
options: Moving a body horizontally on a frictionless surface
explanation: Gravity is vertical. Horizontal displacement → \( W_{gravity} = mgh = 0 \) (no vertical displacement). Work by gravity = 0.
name: Zero Work by Gravity
subject: phy

text: Aryl halides are less reactive than alkyl halides toward nucleophilic substitution because:
options: C–X bond is stronger in aryl halides
Resonance makes C–X bond shorter and stronger (partial double bond character)
explanation: In aryl halides, lone pairs on X overlap with aromatic π system → C–X has partial double bond character → C–X bond shorter/stronger → harder to break.
name: Aryl Halide Reactivity
subject: che

text: The linear thermal expansion formula is:
explanation: Linear expansion: \( \Delta L = L_0\alpha\Delta T \), so \( L = L_0(1 + \alpha\Delta T) \). \(\alpha\) = coefficient of linear expansion.
name: Linear Thermal Expansion
subject: phy

text: Two lines with slopes \( m_1 \) and \( m_2 \) are perpendicular if:
explanation: Lines are perpendicular when \( m_1 \cdot m_2 = -1 \).
name: Perpendicular Lines
subject: maths

text: The sphere with center \((a,b,c)\) and radius \(r\) has equation:
explanation: Sphere equation: \( (x-a)^2+(y-b)^2+(z-c)^2=r^2 \)
name: Equation of Sphere
subject: maths

text: Two lines \(a_1x+b_1y+c_1=0\) and \(a_2x+b_2y+c_2=0\) are parallel if:
explanation: Parallel condition: \( \frac{a_1}{a_2} = \frac{b_1}{b_2} \neq \frac{c_1}{c_2} \), i.e., \( a_1b_2 - a_2b_1 = 0 \).
name: Parallel Lines Condition
subject: maths

text: \( \int \frac{1}{x}\,dx = \)
explanation: \( \int \frac{1}{x}\,dx = \ln|x|+C \). Special case of power rule when \(n=-1\).
name: Integral of 1/x
subject: maths

text: The function \( f(x) = |x| \) is:
explanation: \( f(-x) = |-x| = |x| = f(x) \). So \(f\) is an even function.
name: Even or Odd Function
subject: maths

text: Resonance stabilisation is maximum when:
explanation: Resonance stabilisation is greatest when all contributing structures have equal (or similar) energies. Equal structures → more stabilisation.
name: Resonance Stabilisation
subject: che

text: Amines react with acyl chloride (RCOCl) to give:
explanation: R'NH₂ + RCOCl → RCONHR' + HCl. Nucleophilic acyl substitution. Acylation of amine (Schotten-Baumann conditions).
name: Acylation of Amines
subject: che

text: The rectangular hyperbola has eccentricity:
explanation: Rectangular hyperbola: \( a=b \Rightarrow c=\sqrt{a^2+a^2}=a\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow e = c/a = \sqrt{2} \).
name: Rectangular Hyperbola
subject: maths

text: Which of the following is a factor of \( x^3 - 7x + 6 \)?
explanation: Test \(x=2\): \( 8 - 14 + 6 = 0 \). So \( (x-2) \) is a factor by Factor Theorem.
name: Factor Theorem Application
subject: maths

text: A rocket works on the principle of:
explanation: Rocket propulsion: burning gases ejected backward (action) → rocket moves forward (reaction). Based on Newton's 3rd law and conservation of momentum.
name: Rocket Propulsion
subject: phy

text: Kolbe's reaction (Kolbe-Schmitt) converts sodium phenoxide to:
explanation: Kolbe-Schmitt: sodium phenoxide + CO₂ (high pressure, 125°C) → sodium salicylate → salicylic acid (aspirin precursor).
name: Kolbe's Reaction
subject: che

text: If \( A \) is a symmetric matrix, then \( A^T = \)
explanation: A matrix is symmetric if \( A^T = A \) (transpose equals itself).
name: Symmetric Matrix
subject: maths
